metric_value: $2-3 billion (conservative estimate, includes international sales)
notes: US is the most active and prolific market; Europe is second most active

metric_value: Up to 50% per year for suppliers keeping pace with multi-tier and Internet demands
notes: Applies to ADE suppliers meeting user demands for multi-tier and Internet deployment
Three distinct cost tiers for Application Development Environments

metric_name: Competitive dynamics in niche toolset markets
metric_value: Up to 10 suppliers can flourish at medium size ($40-300M revenues) in hot toolset markets
notes: Suppliers report meeting only 1-2 competitors in any given sales situation despite customers evaluating 3-4 tools

metric_value: Java being adopted for e-commerce applications distributed application deployment and component development
notes: First place where new technology integrates into enterprise; server-side Java focus

metric_value: Java will not replace C++ as primary language within next 2-3 years; will be used for new-application development especially server-side
notes: Prediction: Java supplements rather than replaces C/C++ in enterprise production code

metric_value: Java did gain server dominance but C++ remained primary for performance-critical applications
notes: Actual outcome: Java did gain server dominance but C++ remained primary for performance-critical applications

metric_value: Windows NT is the de facto development environment displacing Unix and C regardless of deployment target
notes: Corporate developers moving to NT as development environment even when deploying to other platforms

metric_value: Microsoft DCOM will by default become primary means of communications between objects; CORBA will bridge to DCOM on client-side
notes: Prediction: DCOM wins on front-end; CORBA survives for backend data access; bridge between the two as best architecture

metric_value: Microsoft succeeded on client but .NET replaced COM/DCOM; CORBA declined on both tiers
notes: Partially correct: Microsoft succeeded on client but .NET replaced COM/DCOM; CORBA declined on both tiers

metric_value: Heavy consolidation expected in Internet development/deployment tool market within next 3 years; startups will be acquired by database/heavy hitters or disappear due to lack of differentiation
notes: Prediction for 1998-2001 timeframe

metric_value: Dot-com bust 2000-2001 confirmed rapid consolidation; many pure-play Internet tool vendors disappeared or were acquired
notes: Dot-com bust 2000-2001 confirmed rapid consolidation; many pure-play Internet tool vendors disappeared or were acquired

metric_value: Y2K remediation is cutting back time and resources available for new development initiatives
notes: Organizations remediating existing code rather than building new applications; post-Y2K re-architecture expected

metric_value: Components take twice as long as expected, cost three times more, 3 years for ROI, deliver less than expected when done
notes: Despite caveats Aberdeen recommends components for long-term flexibility and scalability

metric_value: Automated testing can reduce application testing cycle and cost by 50-75% compared to manual processes
notes: Growing segment of testing tools market is Windows; testers using PCs as low-cost test environments

metric_value: UML is becoming language of choice for OO modeling; most development tool suppliers now supporting and implementing specification
notes: UML proposed by Rational; backed by Oracle and Microsoft; broad industry adoption underway

metric_value: Delivers excellent technology but struggles with enterprise credibility; JBuilder licensed by Oracle; renaming to Inprise June 1998; will never again compete with Microsoft head-to-head
notes: Changing name and focus to middleware market; long-term viability for dev tools but limited enterprise reach

metric_value: Microsoft has largest number of programmers using its tools and largest third-party ISV ecosystem; will remain key contender
notes: Main challenge: upgrade toolsets to deliver more application scalability and robustness for NT and Internet environments

metric_value: Developer/2000 has widest range of scalable application development capabilities available; largest installed base; larger sales force than competitors
notes: Future challenges: improved ease of use for departmental developers; better distributed object support

metric_value: Market-leading modeling tool (Rose); SQA and Pure/Atria acquisitions give testing and SCM portfolio; Microsoft relationship gives strategic credibility; needs coherent integration strategy
notes: Microsoft Visual Modeler is a subset of Rational Rose; portability of Repository planned for non-Microsoft platforms

metric_value: Financial difficulties obscure broad product suite with loyal customers; PowerBuilder being revamped for component support; must reassure customers of long-term viability
notes: Least database-tied of all database-vendor tool suites due to Powersoft's independent origins

metric_value: Harmony unification strategy (OO repository + multimedia GUI) to unite disparate products; delivery planned 1999; Jasmine OO database and Opal legacy tool as key development plays
notes: CA sells tools bundled with solutions rather than standalone; vision and resources to remain key player

metric_value: PVCS is undisputed market leader in version control; Intersolv diversifying into Y2K services and data connectivity to reduce PVCS revenue dependence
notes: Year 2000 consulting expected to expand installed base for PVCS

metric_value: One of largest and most successful testing solution suppliers; LoadRunner for web/client-server load testing; needs better cross-product integration and common repository
notes: Continued dominance predicted; web-based stress testing growth as key success factor

metric_value: Java Workshop and Java language ownership gives immense market credibility; needs Windows marketplace partnerships; database vendors announcing Java VM inclusion signals Java's growing enterprise importance
notes: Java VM inclusion in databases by multiple RDBMS vendors signals strategic importance shift

metric_value: Successful high-end enterprise distributed computing ADE; weathered client-server to Java transition well; needs more Internet and component development support
notes: Client-server to Java transition navigated relatively well; focus on reliable scalable applications and legacy integration

metric_value: Component-based development will be a critical programming technique for next 5-10 years; Java and legacy code expected to provide the largest number of components
notes: Not a large number of component assembly tools available in 1998; growth expected as Java popularity grows

metric_value: IS buyers increasingly looking to development toolsets to implement Intranets and E-commerce; E-commerce is fastest-growing segment of the development-tool market
notes: Three types of e-commerce web presence: static marketing sites; dynamic interactive sites; web upgrades of existing client-server apps

metric_value: Indirect channels continue as primary sales source for ADE suppliers; SIs and application providers buy tools for implementation and integration work
notes: Systems integrators and application providers as key channel partners for development tool vendors

metric_value: Frameworks show great potential for development cost savings but presently slow to sell; users view initial solutions as complex and proprietary
notes: Bundles of APIs and components for cross-platform development; write once deploy many value proposition not yet proven
